articles.full_text:
- Rajasthan Heatwave Warning: Temperature To Rise | 8 Districts Under Alert
8 districts under heatwave warning in Rajasthan:Hot and humid conditions intensify, temperature set to rise again
- Copy link
In Rajasthan, the heat remained normal on Saturday. The maximum temperature in most cities was recorded as normal or slightly below. In Sikar, Hanumangarh, Jalore, and Sirohi, the temperature remained below 40 degrees Celsius.
In Bikaner, Churu, Barmer, and Jaisalmer, the maximum temperature was recorded between 40 and 43 degrees Celsius. The Meteorological Department has predicted an increase in heat in the state from next week.
Meanwhile, a yellow alert for heatwave has been issued in 8 districts of the state for Sunday.
Pictures of Rajasthan weather
Weather in the last 24 hours
In the areas of Sirohi, Udaipur, Rajsamand, and Ajmer, the sun was a bit weak during the day, while in the western Rajasthan districts of Barmer, Jaisalmer, Phalodi, and Jodhpur, there was strong sunshine. These districts also experienced the effects of a heatwave. On Saturday, Phalodi recorded the highest temperature, with a maximum of 43.2 degrees Celsius. In Barmer, the maximum temperature was 42.8 degrees.
What will the weather be like next
According to the weather center, there is no possibility of rain for 4 days. Heat will also increase during the heatwave. The weather will remain clear in most districts of the state, and the maximum temperature is expected to rise rapidly.
How was the weather in the major cities of Rajasthan
Jaipur: Intense heat continued on Saturday as the temperature rose by 1.7 degrees. The maximum temperature was recorded at 41.4°C, while the minimum rose to 28.8°C. Similar weather conditions are expected for the next 3–4 days.
Jodhpur: Light clouds covered the city on Saturday, bringing a slight dip in temperature, but humidity continued to trouble people. The daytime temperature remained above 40°C, while the minimum temperature hovered around 30°C.
Kota: Hot winds troubled people throughout the day, forcing many to cover their faces while stepping out. Some relief came in the evening as the sun weakened, though humidity began increasing. The night temperature rose by 2 degrees to 30°C.
Ajmer: Humidity and hot winds increased discomfort in the city. The Meteorological Department recorded a maximum temperature of 40.9°C and a minimum of 28.3°C. Patients and their relatives at JLN Hospital are struggling with the heat, with many bringing fans from home for relief.
Udaipur: The Lake City had been witnessing severe heat for the past two days. After touching 41.1°C on May 15, the temperature dropped to 40°C on Saturday. Cloudy skies and cool evening breezes brought major relief. The minimum temperature was recorded at 24°C.
Alwar: Strong sunlight and hot winds continued to intensify the heatwave effect. The day temperature remained at 43°C. The Meteorological Department has predicted clear weather for the next two days, which may further increase the heat.
Sikar: Clear skies and strong sunlight dominated the day, while humidity persisted throughout. The weather is expected to remain dry for the next four days, with no immediate relief from the heat. The maximum temperature was 41°C and the minimum 23.3°C.
